believes that employees should have opportunities to enjoy time away from work to help balance their lives. For this

updated regarding the status of your return at all times. If your supervisor is unavailable when you call, contact Human

Sick Time
Sick time is time away from work when you or one of your family members is sick or has a physician and dental
appointment.
You are not required to give any specific reason for using your sick time. However, when you plan to use time for
scheduled appointments, you must notify your supervisor as far in advance as possible.

Regardless of whether the time is compensable, off-hour phone related activities are prohibited for nonexempt
employees.
Please direct questions or concerns regarding this policy to Human Resources.
ground_truth: Submit time-off requests to your supervisor with as much advance notice as possible. Requests are subject to approval based on staffing needs. PTO requests are evaluated under the PTO Policy; vacation requests must be submitted in writing at least one month before the desired start under the Vacation Policy.

question: What is the process for an internal transfer or promotion?
answer:
According to our Internal Transfer/Promotion Policy, the process for an internal transfer or promotion is as follows:

1. The employee must complete the Internal Transfer/Promotion Application form and submit it to Human Resources with their manager's signature (Source 1: Internal Transfer/Promotion Policy, Section: Employment Policies).
2. After the interview process is complete, if the employee is selected for the position, they will receive documentation and the transition process will begin (Source 3: Internal Transfer/Promotion Policy, Section: Employment Policies).

Please note that transfers will be made in a manner that disrupts ongoing operations as little as possible, and employees should not be approached by management without the knowledge of their manager and Human Resources (Source 1: Internal Transfer/Promotion Policy, Section: Employment Policies).

question: How do I report an ethics violation?
answer:
According to our Code of Ethics Policy, Section: Workplace Conduct, you can report a violation of this policy. If you suspect a management personnel is failing to report a violation of this policy by their subordinate, you can also report this incident.

To report an ethics violation, please refer to our Code of Ethics Policy, Section: Workplace Conduct.

If an employee is accused of violating antitrust laws, yet he or she did consult legal counsel and acted in good faith, the
employee may not face disciplinary action under this policy. The Company may also assist in the employee’s defense,
within the confines of the law.

Petty slights, annoyances and isolated incidents (unless extremely serious) will not rise to the level of illegality. To be
unlawful, the conduct must create a work environment that would be intimidating, hostile or offensive to reasonable
people.
Offensive conduct may include, but is not limited to, offensive jokes, slurs, epithets or name-calling, physical assaults or
threats, intimidation, ridicule or mockery, insults or put-downs, offensive objects or pictures, and interference with work

An explanation of the incident and the date the incident occurred

Suggestions for ways to resolve the problem

A copy of the immediate supervisor’s written response or a summary of his or her verbal response and the date
when the employee met with the supervisor. If the supervisor provided no response, this should be stated.

ground_truth: Under the Code of Ethics Policy, employees who learn of payments or conduct that may violate the policy must report immediately to their supervisor or the company’s legal counsel. Management receiving a report will promptly involve legal counsel for further investigation.

question: What should I do if I need emergency leave?
answer: According to [Source 3 | Policy: Family and Medical Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk], in cases of emergency, you (or your representative, if you are incapacitated) should give verbal notice as soon as possible, and the application form should be completed as soon as practical.
contexts:
Upon discovering a medical emergency, call 911.
o
Call the receptionist and page an emergency announcement, if possible.
o
Stay with the ill or injured person, being careful not to come into contact with any body fluids unless properly
trained and protected.
o
Send one person to alert Human Resources so they can notify family members of the ill or injured person.
o
Employees in the immediate vicinity of the emergency, but not involved in the emergency effort, should leave
the area.

Unable to Return to Work
If the employee is not medically released to return to work at the end of his or her leave, employment ends effective the
last day of the approved leave. Exceptions will be made if continued leave is granted as an accommodation under the
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA). Other exemptions may also be granted at management’s discretion. Each situation
will be reviewed on an individual basis.

ground_truth: For FMLA, give verbal notice as soon as possible in emergencies and complete required forms when practical. For general absences, notify your supervisor immediately per sick time and attendance expectations; follow up with documentation as required.

question: How do I file for workers compensation?
answer: According to our Workers’ Compensation Policy (Section: Appendix), employees must report any work-related injury or disease immediately (or as soon as practicable) to their supervisor and Human Resources so that the necessary paperwork can be completed in a timely manner.

question: What is the onboarding process for new hires?
answer:
According to our Internal Transfer/Promotion Policy (Source 1), when you are selected for the position, you will receive documentation and the transition process will begin. However, the policy does not provide a detailed description of the onboarding process.

To provide a more comprehensive answer, I would recommend reviewing our Onboarding Policy, which is not provided in the given context. If you would like, I can suggest that you contact HR to inquire about the onboarding process.

However, I can provide some general information about what typically occurs during the onboarding process. This may include:

- Reviewing company policies and procedures
- Completing necessary paperwork and documentation
- Meeting with HR and other team members
- Participating in training and orientation sessions
- Setting up benefits and other employee accounts

Please note that this is not an exhaustive list, and the actual onboarding process may vary depending on the company and the specific role.

ground_truth: New employees undergo the first 90 days as an orientation period with training and orientation as directed, performance monitoring, and an evaluation at the end. New hires also complete handbook acknowledgment, benefits enrollment (coverage begins the first of the month following hire when forms are completed), and other standard employment paperwork.

question: How do I request a reasonable accommodation for a disability?
answer: To request a reasonable accommodation for a disability, please contact the Americans with Disabilities Policy team at (see Source 2 | Policy: Americans with Disabilities Policy | Section: Employment Policies | Type: policy_chunk) for assistance. They will be able to guide you through the process and address any questions or requests for accommodation.

undue hardship or causing a workplace safety risk, he or she will be given the same consideration for employment as any
other applicant.

question: What is the difference between sick leave and personal days?
answer: I don't have enough information in our HR policies to answer that question.
contexts:
Sick time may not be carried over from year to year. If you are away from work for less than half of a day, the option of a
half sick day may be used, or the missed amount of time may be made up during that workweek if the work schedule

Sick time is a benefit provided to you in the event you need to take time off because of your health or the health of a

the FMLA, you may take medical leave under this policy (see “Definitions” for the definition of serious health condition).
A serious health condition generally occurs when you:
o
Receive inpatient care in a hospital, hospice or nursing home
o
Suffer a period of incapacity accompanied by continuing outpatient treatment or care by a health care provider

and eligible.]
Pay Status
An employee who is taking personal leave must exhaust all accrued time off prior to being placed in an unpaid leave
status.
While using any form of paid time off, an employee will continue to accrue time off. When all paid time off banks are
exhausted, the leave will be unpaid. No additional paid time off will be accrued during a period of unpaid leave.
Paid time off accrual will restart upon the employee's return to paid status.
Employee Benefits While on Leave

Confirmed Infection
Employees who are absent due to a confirmed pandemic flu infection will receive full pay for their normally scheduled
work hours until a medical care provided has authorized their return to work. Each employee using Pandemic Flu Leave
will be required to provide written documentation from a medical care provider. If the illness extends beyond 90 days,
employees eligible for the Company’s Long-Term Disability (LTD) plan may qualify for LTD benefits. All plan provisions for

You may also choose to substitute additional paid or unpaid leave that you have accrued.
When you substitute vacation days or other paid leave, the absence will be counted against your entitlement to FMLA
leave under this policy and will not extend your leave. In other words, you are using your paid leave concurrently with
your FMLA leave.

ground_truth: Sick time is for illness and medical/dental appointments for you or a family member, with accrual and a maximum of six days per calendar year after the first full calendar year of full-time employment (subject to earlier half-day monthly accrual). The handbook does not define a separate “personal days” allotment; PTO covers personal business and emergencies and is capped at 40 hours of accrued time.

question: How do exempt and non-exempt employees differ?
answer:
According to our Employee Classification Policy, Section: General Practices, exempt and non-exempt employees differ as follows:

Exempt employees are those who meet any of the FLSA's exemption standards, including managerial, supervisory, professional, sales or administrative employees. (Source 1 | Policy: Employee Classification Policy | Section: General Practices)

Non-exempt employees, on the other hand, are those whose positions do not meet the FLSA exemption standards. (Source 1 | Policy: Employee Classification Policy | Section: General Practices)
contexts:
Employees are classified as either exempt or non-exempt for pay administration purposes, as determined by the federal
Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA).
The definitions of the worker classification categories can be summarized as follows:
Exempt—Employees who meet any of the FLSA’s exemption standards, including managerial, supervisory, professional,
sales or administrative employees.
Non-exempt—Employees whose positions do not meet the FLSA exemption standards. Overtime work is prohibited

portion of the premium. Employees will have an opportunity to qualify for the non-smoking discount at least once per

by different means. An employee should contact Human Resources and Human Resources will work with the employee

Independent contractors - Consultants, freelancers and independent contractors are not employees of . The distinction
between employees and independent contractors is crucial because employees may be entitled to participate in the
company’s benefits programs, while independent contractors are not. In addition, is not required to satisfy income,
Social Security, Medicare or unemployment tax withholdings or payment requirements for independent contractors.

subject to fines and imprisonment. Management will help guide employees in abiding by antitrust decrees applicable to

complies with all applicable laws concerning the employment of individuals with disabilities and acts in accordance with

discharge, compensation, training or other terms, conditions and privileges of employment.
ground_truth: Exempt employees meet FLSA exemption standards (e.g., managerial, professional, administrative). Non-exempt employees do not meet those standards; overtime work is prohibited without supervisor authorization, and non-exempt employees must be paid 1.5× the regular rate for hours over 40 per week.

question: What is the difference between voluntary and involuntary termination?
answer: According to our Employment Termination Policy (Section: Employment Policies), voluntary termination is initiated by the employee (Source 1), whereas involuntary termination is initiated by VanaciPrime (Source 1).
contexts:
Termination of employment is an inevitable part of personnel activity within any organization, and many of the reasons
for termination are routine. Below are examples of some of the most common circumstances under which employment
is terminated:

Resignation—voluntary employment termination initiated by an employee

Termination—involuntary employment termination initiated by

Layoff—involuntary employment termination initiated by VanaciPrime for nondisciplinary reasons

Abuse of this policy will result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. reserves the right to revise this
policy without notice during changing pandemic conditions.

Qualifying events for spouses that allow up to 36 months of benefit continuation:

Covered employee becoming entitled to Medicare

Divorce or legal separation of the covered employee

Death of the covered employee
Qualifying events for dependent children that allow up to 18 months of benefit continuation:

Voluntary or involuntary termination of the covered employee's employment for any reason other than gross
misconduct

Reduction in the hours worked by the covered employee

after disciplinary action takes place, they may be subject to termination.
o
Romantic relationships between supervising, managing or executive employees and subordinates are strictly
prohibited. If a relationship does develop between a supervising employee and his or her subordinate,
management should be notified immediately so that a department transfer may be considered.

Leave approval or denial is done at the discretion of the employee’s supervisor and Human Resources. Personal leave is
not granted for engaging in employment outside of , pursuing an independent business venture or as additional leave
after FMLA (or Non-FMLA) Medical Leave. This leave policy does not allow for intermittent leave.
[EMPLOYER: It is advised that FMLA leave rights be considered and offered first when the employee is FMLA-covered
and eligible.]
Pay Status
ground_truth: Resignation is voluntary termination by the employee; the handbook asks for at least two weeks’ written notice. Involuntary termination is initiated by the company (including layoff for nondisciplinary reasons). Failing to report for three consecutive days without informing management is treated as voluntary resignation.
